 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8kkm","title":"Custom Pack Authoring via External YAML","description":"# Epic: Custom Pack Authoring via External YAML

## Priority Score: 640/1000 (Fourth Priority)

## Background and Rationale

Every organization has domain-specific security needs: internal tools with dangerous flags, company-specific deployment commands, project-specific database operations.

Currently users must fork DCG or wait for upstream changes. External packs enable instant custom policies without code changes, easy sharing within teams, and gradual adoption paths.

## Example Pack Definition

File: ~/.config/dcg/packs/mycompany.yaml

  id: mycompany.policies
  name: MyCompany Security Policies
  version: 1.0.0
  
  destructive_patterns:
    - name: deploy-prod-direct
      pattern: deploy\s+(-e|--env)\s*=?\s*prod
      severity: critical
      description: Direct production deployment
      explanation: |
        Direct production deployments bypass change management.
        Use the deployment pipeline instead.
  
  safe_patterns:
    - name: deploy-staging
      pattern: deploy\s+(-e|--env)\s*=?\s*stag
      description: Staging deployments are allowed
  
  keywords: [deploy, release, publish]

## Technical Approach

1. Define YAML schema mirroring internal Pack struct
2. Add serde_yaml dependency for parsing
3. Create pack loader that reads external files
4. Validate schema and compile patterns on load
5. Merge with built-in packs (custom takes precedence)
6. Add dcg pack validate command

## Configuration

In dcg.toml:
  [packs]
  custom_paths = [
    ~/.config/dcg/packs/*.yaml,
    /etc/dcg/packs/*.yaml
  ]

## Validation Command

dcg pack validate ./mycompany.yaml

Shows:
- Schema validity
- Pattern compilation results  
- Conflicts with core packs
- Suggestions for improvement

## Key Files

- New: src/packs/external.rs - YAML loader
- src/packs/mod.rs - Integration with pack registry
- src/config.rs - custom_paths option
- src/cli.rs - pack validate command

## Success Criteria

- External YAML packs load correctly
- Patterns compile and match as expected
- Validation catches common errors
- Hot reload on file changes (optional v2)
- Clear error messages for invalid packs

## Strategic Value

This transforms DCG from a fixed tool into an extensible platform. Organizations can codify security policies in version-controlled YAML and share packs internally.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:36.171478205-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:36.171478205-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8kkm.1","title":"[Packs] Schema versioning + pack ID collision behavior","description":"# Task: Schema versioning + pack ID collision behavior

## Purpose
Define how external pack schema versions are handled and what happens when
custom pack IDs collide with built-in packs.

## Requirements
- Explicit schema version field in YAML
- Clear rules for ID collisions (override vs reject) with deterministic behavior
- Validation error messaging that is actionable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema version required and validated
- Collision behavior is documented and enforced
- Tests cover both allowed and rejected collision cases

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8kkm.2","title":"[Packs] Regex engine selection rules for custom packs","description":"# Task: Regex engine selection rules for custom packs

## Purpose
Custom pack patterns must use the same compiled regex abstraction as built-ins
so performance and correctness stay consistent.

## Requirements
- Use `CompiledRegex`/`LazyCompiledRegex` for custom patterns
- Enforce backtracking engine only when required
- Validation must report which engine each pattern uses (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Custom patterns compile through the same engine selection path
- No new regex feature regressions

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8kkm.5","title":"[Packs] E2E tests for custom pack validation + loading","description":"# Task: E2E tests for custom pack validation + loading

## Purpose
Ensure end-to-end custom pack flow works: validate → load → evaluate.

## Requirements
- Fixture YAML pack file(s)
- `dcg pack validate` success + failure cases
- Hook/CLI evaluation using custom pack enabled in config
- Verbose logging on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests confirm custom pack influences evaluation
- Validation errors are clear and stable

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","title":"[PACK] backup.borg - BorgBackup Pack","description":"# BorgBackup Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Borg backup operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `borg delete` - Deletes archives or entire repo
- `borg prune` - Removes archives based on retention
- `borg compact` - After delete, reclaims space
- `borg recreate` - Can exclude data (data loss)
- `borg key export` then delete
- `borg break-lock` - Force breaks locks

## Safe Patterns
- `borg list`
- `borg info`
- `borg diff`
- `borg check`
- `borg create` (backup)
- `borg extract` (restore)
- `borg mount`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: backup.* (git_safety_guard-alc)
- Keywords: borg
- Note: prune with wrong retention is common mistake

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Archive deletion blocked
- [ ] Prune operations blocked
- [ ] Repository deletion blocked
- [ ] Create/extract allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common Borg operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:32:51.689713034-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:41:59.506074785-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:41:59.506074785-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.14996292-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.188671791-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.227367778-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.270813031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.311857467-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:31.352077572-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.655715875-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8rw","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.701531034-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","title":"[DESIGN] Architectural Decisions for Pack Implementation","description":"# Architectural Decisions for Pack Implementation

## Overview
Before implementing new packs, several architectural decisions need to be documented and agreed upon. These decisions affect all packs and should be resolved BEFORE implementation begins.

## Decisions to Document

### 1. REST API Pattern Detection Strategy
**Problem**: Many tools use REST APIs via curl/httpie. How do we detect `curl -X DELETE api.example.com/resource`?

**Options**:
A. Keyword-based: Match `curl` + detect DELETE method + known API hostnames
B. AST-based: Parse curl arguments to extract method and URL
C. Hybrid: Quick keyword check, then AST parse for suspected matches

**Considerations**:
- Performance impact of AST parsing
- False positive rate for each approach
- Maintainability of hostname lists

### 2. Pack Overlap Resolution
**Problem**: cicd.github_actions and platform.github both cover `gh` commands

**Options**:
A. Strict separation: Each pack handles distinct commands
B. Layered: Platform pack is base, cicd pack extends
C. Merged: Single github pack with all patterns

**Considerations**:
- User configuration complexity
- Pattern duplication
- Maintenance burden

### 3. Command Alias Handling
**Problem**: `kubectl` vs `k`, `docker` vs `podman`, etc.

**Options**:
A. Explicit aliases: List all known aliases per pack
B. Keyword expansion: User configures their aliases
C. Pattern-based: Use regex to catch common alias patterns

**Considerations**:
- User customization needs
- Performance of expanded keyword lists

### 4. Performance Budget Per Pack
**Problem**: Each pack adds patterns. How do we prevent slowdown?

**Proposal**:
- Maximum patterns per pack: 50
- Maximum regex complexity score: TBD
- Benchmark requirement: <1ms per pack evaluation

### 5. Safe vs Destructive Threshold
**Problem**: Some commands are context-dependent (e.g., `rsync` is safe without --delete)

**Options**:
A. Flag-aware matching: Parse flags to determine safety
B. Conservative: Block ambiguous commands
C. Configurable: User sets their preference

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each decision documented with chosen option and rationale
- [ ] Performance benchmarks established
- [ ] Design doc created in docs/architecture/pack-design.md
- [ ] Team review completed

## Dependencies
- Should be resolved BEFORE implementing Tier 1 packs
- Informs testing strategy","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:53.183447098-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:51:09.662426829-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:51:09.662426829-05:00","close_reason":"Documented pack architecture decisions in docs/architecture/pack-design.md; pending team review","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ltou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:21.471784408-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:21.510516334-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-8u1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qdhh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:35:21.550636035-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f2rj","title":"[Suggest] Integrate with allowlist config writing","description":"# Task: Integrate with allowlist config writing

## Parent Epic: Auto-Suggested Allowlists (git_safety_guard-5apz)

## Objective

When users accept suggested patterns, write them to the appropriate allowlist configuration file with proper formatting and comments.

## Config File Locations

DCG allowlists can be in:
1. Project: .dcg.toml or .dcg/allowlist.toml
2. User: ~/.config/dcg/allowlist.toml
3. System: /etc/dcg/allowlist.toml

Default to user config unless project config exists.

## Output Format

When accepting a pattern, append to config:

# Auto-suggested by dcg suggest-allowlist on 2024-01-15
# Based on 127 blocked commands over 30 days
# Confidence: HIGH, Risk: LOW
[[allowlist.patterns]]
pattern = "npm run (build|test|lint)"
reason = "NPM scripts (build, test, lint) - auto-suggested"

## Implementation Steps

1. Determine target config file (project > user)
2. Read existing config to avoid duplicates
3. Format new entry with metadata comments
4. Append atomically (write temp, rename)
5. Validate result parses correctly

## Conflict Detection

Before writing, check:
- Pattern already exists (skip with message)
- Pattern conflicts with existing block (warn user)
- Pattern is overly broad (suggest refinement)

## Atomic Writing

Use atomic file operations to prevent corruption:

pub fn append_allowlist_entry(
    config_path: &Path,
    entry: &AllowlistEntry,
) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {
    // 1. Read existing content
    // 2. Append new entry
    // 3. Write to temp file
    // 4. Validate temp file parses
    // 5. Atomic rename temp -> target
}

## Rollback Support

If user wants to undo:
- Track session additions in memory
- dcg suggest-allowlist --undo removes last session
- Or manual editing (comments show auto-suggested)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Patterns written to appropriate config file
- Metadata comments included
- Atomic write prevents corruption
- Duplicate detection works
- Manual editing still possible

## Dependencies

- Task: Interactive CLI (provides pattern to write)

## Estimated Effort: 1 day","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:08:25.819997599-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:08:25.819997599-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f2rj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-iwtb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:08:38.882420798-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f2rj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:20:23.768079174-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f2rj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:20:26.905071534-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","title":"[PACK] secrets.vault - HashiCorp Vault Pack","description":"# HashiCorp Vault Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for HashiCorp Vault CLI and API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `vault secrets disable` - Disables secrets engine (data loss)
- `vault kv delete` - Deletes secrets
- `vault kv destroy` - Permanently destroys secret versions
- `vault kv metadata delete` - Deletes all versions
- `vault auth disable` - Disables auth method
- `vault policy delete` - Removes policies
- `vault token revoke` - Revokes tokens
- `vault lease revoke` - Revokes leases

## Safe Patterns
- `vault read`, `vault list`
- `vault kv get`, `vault kv list`
- `vault status`, `vault version`
- `vault audit list`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: vault
- Consider namespace operations for enterprise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All destructive patterns blocked
- [ ] Safe patterns not blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Vault workflows
- [ ] Documentation includes examples","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:00.911143116-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:08.790596553-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:28:08.790596553-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: secrets.vault pack already present in repo (src/packs/secrets/vault.rs + tests) and registered in pack registry. Docs update delegated to mbq7.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.083179678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.124770231-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.167848674-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.208877791-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.24822327-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:35.288944838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.129072418-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f3e","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:10.172995575-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","title":"Implement ast-grep-core pattern matching layer","description":"# ast-grep-core Pattern Matching Layer

## Objective

Integrate ast-grep-core as a Rust library for structural pattern matching in heredoc/inline-script content.

## Architecture decision

Prefer **embedded ast-grep-core** over shelling out to a CLI, because:

- avoids per-invocation process spawn overhead
- keeps a single-binary distribution story
- enables richer match metadata (spans) for `dcg explain`

(Still subject to ADR confirmation; this task implements whichever embedded approach is chosen.)

## Dependencies to add (tentative)

```toml
[dependencies]
ast-grep-core = "0.40"
ast-grep-language = { version = "0.37", features = [
    "bash",
    "python",
    "javascript",
    "typescript",
    "ruby"
    # Note: Perl may not be available
] }
```

## API design

```rust
/// Pattern matcher using ast-grep-core
pub struct AstMatcher {
    patterns: HashMap<Language, Vec<CompiledPattern>>,
}

impl AstMatcher {
    /// Create matcher with default destructive patterns
    pub fn new() -> Self;

    /// Load patterns from config / registry
    pub fn from_config(config: &PatternConfig) -> Result<Self>;

    /// Match patterns against code (bounded)
    pub fn find_matches(&self, code: &str, language: Language) -> Result<Vec<PatternMatch>, MatchError>;
}

/// Result of pattern matching
pub struct PatternMatch {
    pub rule_id: String,             // stable: pack_id:pattern_name
    pub reason: String,
    pub matched_text_preview: String,
    pub start: usize,                // byte offset (or row/col)
    pub end: usize,
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub severity: Severity,          // ties into decision policy
}

pub enum Severity { Error, Warning, Info }
```

## Pattern definition format (example)

Patterns should be defined and reviewed under `git_safety_guard-6sg` (pattern library structure). The matching layer must be agnostic to *what* the patterns are, but must support:

- stable IDs
- per-pattern severity
- per-pattern suggestions (optional)

## Error handling / fail-open

In hook mode:

- Unsupported language → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Parse error → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker
- Timeout → skip analysis (allow) + trace marker

In `dcg test` / `dcg explain` / `dcg scan`:

- return structured diagnostics (not silent skip)

## Performance targets (initial)

- Pattern compilation: one-time at startup (bounded)
- Parse: <2ms for typical heredoc sizes
- Match: <1ms typical
- Total AST path: bounded by a hard timeout

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implements an embedded AST matcher that can:
  - parse at least one supported language
  - match at least one simple pattern
  - return match spans + line number
- Enforces a hard timeout for AST work and fails open in hook mode on timeout.
- Produces match metadata compatible with:
  - `dcg explain` highlighting (matched span + preview)
  - rule attribution (stable rule_id)
  - decision policy (severity)
- Includes unit tests for:
  - a positive match
  - a negative (safe) snippet
  - an unsupported-language path
  - a timeout path (if testable)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive control
This layer must support the FP-immunity strategy, not undermine it:

- matchers should attach stable rule IDs + severity so policy can default-warn vs deny
- matchers should support negative fixtures / “must-not-match” tests per rule family
- prefer composite/high-signal rules (AST match + literal destructive payload) over broad matches (e.g., `exec($VAR)`)

### Tests
- For each supported language: at least one positive and one negative snippet.
- Span/line mapping test: matches return accurate location for explain output.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:24.313325332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T02:41:21.25882879-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ast-grep-core pattern matching layer with AstMatcher struct, find_matches(), timeout handling, and 17 unit tests. All acceptance criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5ib","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.909572665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-du4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:32:32.929896409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:18.965756128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-fjyh","title":"[Packs] Add custom_paths config option and documentation","description":"# Task: Add custom_paths config option and documentation

## Parent Epic: Custom Pack Authoring (git_safety_guard-8kkm)

## Objective

Add the configuration option for specifying external pack paths and create comprehensive documentation for custom pack authoring.

## Config Option

In src/config.rs, add to PacksConfig:

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Default)]
pub struct PacksConfig {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    /// Paths to external pack YAML files.
    /// Supports glob patterns. Paths are checked in order.
    /// Example: ["~/.config/dcg/packs/*.yaml"]
    #[serde(default)]
    pub custom_paths: Vec<String>,
}

## Default Paths

If custom_paths is empty, check default locations:
1. .dcg/packs/*.yaml (project-local)
2. ~/.config/dcg/packs/*.yaml (user)

## Path Expansion

- ~ expands to home directory
- Glob patterns (*.yaml) supported
- Relative paths relative to config file location

## Documentation

Create docs/custom-packs.md:

# Creating Custom Packs

Custom packs let you add organization-specific patterns...

## Quick Start

1. Create a YAML file:
   [example pack]

2. Validate it:
   $ dcg pack validate mypack.yaml

3. Add to config:
   [packs]
   custom_paths = ["~/.config/dcg/packs/*.yaml"]

## Schema Reference

[full schema documentation]

## Best Practices

- Use specific patterns over broad wildcards
- Include explanations for user clarity
- Test patterns with dcg test before deploying
- Version your packs in source control

## Examples

[multiple example packs for common use cases]

## Integration with README

Add section to main README pointing to custom packs documentation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Config option documented in generated sample config
- custom_paths properly loaded and expanded
- Documentation comprehensive and beginner-friendly
- Example packs included

## Dependencies

- Task: Pack loader (uses config option)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-foj","title":"[PACK] cdn.cloudfront - AWS CloudFront Pack","description":"# AWS CloudFront CDN Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS CloudFront operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### aws CLI
- `aws cloudfront delete-distribution`
- `aws cloudfront delete-cache-policy`
- `aws cloudfront delete-origin-request-policy`
- `aws cloudfront delete-function`
- `aws cloudfront create-invalidation` (costly, can affect caching)

### Origin Changes
- `aws cloudfront update-distribution` with origin removal

## Safe Patterns
- `aws cloudfront list-distributions`
- `aws cloudfront get-distribution`
- `aws cloudfront get-distribution-config`
- `aws cloudfront list-invalidations`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cdn.* (git_safety_guard-wso)
- Keywords: aws, cloudfront
- Note: Invalidations have cost implications

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for distribution deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for policy deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/cdn_cloudfront.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Distribution deletion blocked
- [ ] Policy deletion blocked
- [ ] List/get operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:16.954912451-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:19:41.184858034-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:19:41.184858034-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented cdn.cloudfront pack with 12 safe patterns and 7 destructive patterns covering distributions, cache policies, functions, and invalidations","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.264189343-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.30989559-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.355790301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.402243183-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.447889247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:43.495160869-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.088050472-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-foj","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.133330634-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","title":"Implement false positive reduction for string arguments","description":"# False Positive Reduction Implementation

## Problem

Current regex patterns match dangerous strings even in safe contexts:
- `bd create --description="This blocks rm -rf"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `git commit -m "Fix rm -rf pattern"` → BLOCKED (wrong!)
- `grep "rm -rf" patterns.txt` → BLOCKED (wrong!)

## Solution

Parse command structure to identify execution context before applying destructive patterns.

## Implementation

### 1. Safe Command Registry

```rust
static SAFE_STRING_ARG_COMMANDS: &[(&str, &[&str])] = &[
    ("bd", &["--description", "--title", "-d", "-t"]),
    ("git", &["commit -m", "tag -m", "notes add -m"]),
    ("grep", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("rg", &["-e", "--regexp"]),
    ("echo", &[]),  // all args are strings
    ("printf", &[]),
];
```

### 2. Execution Context Detection

```rust
enum ExecutionContext {
    Direct,           // Command itself is executed
    StringArgument,   // Inside quoted string argument to safe command
    HeredocBody,      // Inside heredoc (needs Tier 2 analysis)
    PipeTarget,       // Right side of pipe (executed)
    CommandSubst,     // Inside $() or backticks (executed)
}

fn detect_execution_context(cmd: &str, position: usize) -> ExecutionContext;
```

### 3. Integration Point

Modify main check flow:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Check if dangerous patterns are in safe context
    if is_safe_string_context(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // Continue with heredoc detection and pattern matching...
}
```

## Test Cases

### Must ALLOW (false positive prevention)
- `bd create --description="Pattern blocks rm -rf commands"`
- `git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"`
- `grep "rm -rf" src/patterns.rs`
- `echo "example: git push --force"`
- `rg -e "DROP TABLE" --type sql`

### Must BLOCK (true positives preserved)
- `rm -rf /tmp/*`
- `git reset --hard HEAD~5`
- `bash -c "rm -rf /"`
- `echo "hi" | bash -c "rm -rf /"`

## Performance

- < 100 microseconds additional overhead
- Simple string prefix matching for command detection
- No regex needed for this layer

## Dependencies

- Depends on: False positive design (2cu)

## Acceptance Criteria

- The documented false-positive examples are allowed in hook mode and `dcg test`.
- True-positive executions are still blocked, including destructive strings inside:
  - `bash -c ...`
  - `sh -c ...`
  - `node -e ...` (where the code executes)
  - command substitution (`$(...)` / backticks)
- Context classification is conservative:
  - only skips matching inside explicitly-registered safe string arguments
  - does not treat unknown flags/commands as safe
- Performance overhead is bounded (target < 100µs additional overhead on typical allow-path commands).
","notes":"## Objective

Implement the execution-context + safe-string-arg behavior described in `git_safety_guard-t8x` so dcg stops blocking documentation/testing workflows (commit messages, bd descriptions, grep/rg patterns) while still blocking real executions.

## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

(Kept here so the task remains self-contained even though the description starts with Problem/Solution.)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleRobin","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:52:22.183030324-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:52:22.183030324-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented safe-string-arg sanitizer + integrated into hook/evaluator; added unit/E2E regression coverage","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2cu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.226122941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:02:22.808621071-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:42.899745326-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:47.932828938-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fpim","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:03:52.966413813-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","title":"Implement docker-compose extractor","description":"## Objective

Implement an extractor for docker-compose files to detect executable command contexts.

## File Pattern

- `docker-compose.yml`
- `docker-compose.yaml`
- `compose.yml`
- `compose.yaml`

## Extraction Targets

### Command Blocks (executed)
- `command:` values
- `entrypoint:` values
- `healthcheck.test:` commands

### NOT Extracted (data only)
- `environment:` values
- `labels:` values
- Volume paths
- Comments

## Example

```yaml
services:
  app:
    command: /bin/sh -c "rm -rf /data/*"  # EXTRACT THIS
    environment:
      CLEANUP: "rm -rf /"  # DO NOT extract
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Correctly extracts command/entrypoint with line numbers
- Handles both string and array formats
- Ignores environment variables and labels
- Unit tests with representative fixtures","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive controls
- Extract only from executed compose fields (e.g., `command`, `entrypoint`) and avoid scanning:
  - environment variables
  - comments
  - labels/metadata
- Include negative fixtures for dangerous strings in env/labels.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:24:57.298774964-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:56.093978239-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T13:11:56.093978239-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: docker-compose extractor with 9 tests. Extracts command/entrypoint/healthcheck.test, correctly ignores environment/labels/volumes/ports. 701 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:25:20.580453194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-fu8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:46:02.44142759-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
